text: In this video, we will present the laparoscopic repair of left para duodenal hernia. Para duodenal hernia is a type of internal abdominal hernias and occurs when the small bowel herniates into the left or right para duodenal fossa. The clinical manifestation can be ranged from recurrent abdominal pain attacks to acute intestinal obstruction with or without strangulation. Although para duodenal hernias have unique computed tomography appearance, the diagnosis of para duodenal hernias can be missed in emergency settings. The only treatment option is surgery. During surgery, the herniated small bowel loops are reduced and the hernia orifice is closed.
In the majority of previous reports, open approach was used for hernia repair, however laparoscopic repair is also possible when the diagnosis is made preoperatively.

The video shows a computed tomography scan revealing a sac-like cluster of small bowel loops in the hernia sac located on the left side of the fourth part of the duodenum, posterior to the transverse colon. Fluid and congestion are visible within the sac.

The surgeon retracts the transverse mesocolon caudally to expose the hernia orifice located on the left side of the duodenal-jejunal junction. The incarcerated small bowel loops are carefully reduced into the peritoneal cavity, extending just beyond the fourth part of the duodenum. No signs of small bowel ischemia are observed.

The surgeon evaluates the hernia sac, observing a small amount of serohemorrhagic fluid. Using a laparoscopic suturing device, a non-absorbable suture is applied in a purse string method to close the hernia orifice, taking serosal bites from the jejunal segment.

Following the control of the closure of the orifice, operation was completed. After surgery, patient was discharged on post-operative third day without any complication and he has not experienced any complaints in two-year follow-up period. As a conclusion, laparoscopic surgery can be used as a feasible and safe approach in the treatment of left para-duodenal hernia.
